shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = W,_,W,L  -> cmplog and value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE, naive BLOCKS, value_profile NON-DECISIVE. Pure i2s-pro family: I2S (input-to-state substitution) is the resolving technique; the value-profile gradient adds nothing decisive.

hypotheses.label: I2S enriches the cmplog corpus with the exact CMP-target byte(s) at the gate offset (literal substitution), which naive's blind havoc cannot place

params.gate_target_value: per-branch W (CMP-target) byte(s) at the fixed gate offset
params.gate_decoy_value: per-branch L (loser-occupying) byte(s) at the same offset

hypotheses.prediction: signed_target_enrich > 0: the cmplog corpus is log2-enriched (vs naive) for the exact target byte value at the fixed gate offset. DISCRIMINATING because an i2s-pro literal-substitution mechanism deterministically plants the CMP operand at that one offset, so its corpus concentrates on the target value; a NON-i2s explanation (naive reaches it by coverage gradient, or the gate is not an I2S value gate) predicts signed_target_enrich ~ 0. G2-independent: enrichment is measured on the saved CORPUS bytes at the gate offset, not on which fuzzer resolved.

hypotheses.label: I2S repairs interdependent multi-field structure (ELF section table / PNG iTXt chunk grammar / XML scheme+attribute parse) so the winner corpus drives the gated loop/branch line DEEPER per seed than the loser, rather than flipping a single fixed-offset literal

params.branch_line: per-branch gate line (bloaty section_count loop guard; libpng png_handle_iTXt inner loops L2767/L2796/L2810; libxml2 xmlParse3986Authority L536 / attribute property-list walk L2540)

hypotheses.prediction: depth_lift > 1 AND winner_deeper=true: the winner-resolving seeds execute the gated loop/branch line MORE per seed than the loser-blocking seeds, because I2S repaired enough of the surrounding multi-field structure (a valid ELF section table that makes section_count()>1 and the loop iterate; a parseable iTXt chunk that survives the compression-info gate and reaches the inner NUL-scan loops; a 'http://[' authority that drives xmlParse3986Host; an attribute-rich document that forces the property-list walk) for the parser to advance through the structure. DISCRIMINATING: a pure single-literal-substitution alternative would flip the branch with EQUAL reach (one byte changes the comparison outcome, not how deeply the loop iterates), predicting depth_lift ~ 1; a STRUCTURAL-assembly mechanism predicts the winner reaches the gate region substantially deeper per seed (loop body executes; loser never enters). This also rules out operand_enrichment as the operative signal here, since these gates have NO single fixed offset and dispersed/different-sized W vs L seeds (operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature). G2-independent: depth_lift is a per-seed-normalized execution-count ratio at the branch line from llvm-cov, computed from seed bytes through the cov binary, NOT from which fuzzer resolved the branch in the campaign.

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence 'I2S event' descriptor is proposed (I2S is untagged in mutation_op).
value_profile is treated as a non-decisive feedback for this shape (vp = '_'); no descriptor attributes a gradient mutation to it.

byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is applied ONLY to fixed-offset, byte-aligned value gates; the structural / different-sized-seed gates are routed to depth_reach instead, per the data_reality that byte-alignment fails on different-sized winning vs blocking seeds.

Corpus/seed tools score only targets whose corpus exists on the OWNING server (curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 on s4; lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty on sB). Branches are NOT marked decidable:false for absent-on-s4 corpus; the owning server arbitrates its own targets.
depth_reach needs the target cov image + resolving/blocking seeds in db/blockers.sqlite; libpng, libxml2, and bloaty have those on sB (the 286 reps carry seeds).

notes.priors_kept: KEPT the single prior hypothesis i2s_fixed_offset_literal_substitution VERBATIM (same descriptor, metrics, and rule signed_target_enrich >= 1.0) so the 37 confirmed branches re-validate. I additionally listed under applies_to_branches the unlabeled FIXED-OFFSET literal branches whose only reason for the s4 'rule_not_met' was that their corpus (libpng/libxml2) is owned by sB, not s4 -- they are the SAME literal-substitution mechanism and the owning server's arbiter will score them with operand_enrichment (no new mechanism needed): libpng_3887 (PLTE FOURCC at chunk-name), libpng_3902 (tRNS FOURCC), libpng_3892_h0 (color_type 0x03 at 0x19), libpng_3965_h0 (gAMA length byte at 0x23), libpng_3820 (chunk-type FOURCC at 0x35), libxml2_6205 (non-xmlns prefix), libxml2_6359_h0 (3-byte UTF-8 BOM at 0-2), libxml2_6597_h1 (tab 0x09 in comment body), libxml2_6909 (colon at QName start), libxml2_6946 ('xml' 3-byte prefix).
notes.new_mechanism: ADDED ONE new, genuinely-different hypothesis i2s_structural_assembly_depth for the unlabeled STRUCTURAL multi-field gates that operand_enrichment cannot score (dispersed_multibyte / derived_integer signatures, different-sized W vs L seeds, no single fixed offset). Mechanism: I2S repairs CORRELATED structure (ELF section-count header fields making the loop iterate; an iTXt chunk that survives the compression-info gate to reach the inner NUL-scan loops; the 'http://[' authority prefix that drives xmlParse3986Host to ret!=0; the attribute-rich parser state forcing the property-list walk) so the winner corpus reaches the gate region DEEPER per seed. Measured by the BUILT depth_reach tool (depth_lift / winner_deeper). This is distinct from literal substitution (single byte, equal reach -> depth_lift ~ 1) and the prediction is falsifiable + discriminating (G1). Members: bloaty_87, bloaty_90 (section_count loop guards), libpng_4051/4055_h0/4061_h0 (png_handle_iTXt grammar + inner loops), libxml2_6976 (URI authority depth), libxml2_7287 (attribute property-list walk loop).
notes.decidable_false_subgroup: Marked the once-per-document NAMESPACE-PRESENCE gates decidable:false: libxml2_6930 and libxml2_7250 (xmlFreeDoc / xmlTextReaderFreeDoc: 'if (cur->oldNs != NULL)'). These fire ONCE per parsed document, so depth_reach's depth_lift is degenerate (no loop / single execution); the only available signal is the blocked-side count (side_lift), which is ~tautological with the resolve pattern that DEFINED the shape (winner takes the non-NULL side, loser never does) -- using it would violate G2 (verifying I2S-resolves with I2S-resolves). The discriminating distinction (was oldNs populated because I2S repaired namespace LITERALS, vs because the whole-document parse simply advanced) is not separable by any built tool here: there is no single fixed gate offset for operand_enrichment, no operand literal for value_distance_reached, and no iteration depth for depth_reach. Honest inconclusive is the correct outcome; the fallback carries them.
notes.scope_and_caveats: depth_reach is Docker-per-branch (~30-60s) and needs the libpng/libxml2/bloaty cov images on sB; any structural-assembly member whose cov image or >=1 W/>=1 L seed is missing falls to the inconclusive fallback automatically. depth_lift threshold 1.5 is conservative relative to depth_reach's validated iteration-depth separation (anchor harfbuzz/6585, 7/20 confirmed) and pairs with the categorical winner_deeper guard so noise near 1.0 does not produce a spurious label. No further NEW descriptor is proposed: the two built tools (operand_enrichment for fixed-offset literals, depth_reach for structural-assembly depth) cover every discriminable hypothesis in this shape; the remaining non-discriminable presence gates are honestly left to inconclusive.
